**Files:** src/daemon/repl/server.rs

title: Phase 6: Checkpoint export algorithm
description:
**Problem**
bd-3m5.20 is too large. Split: export algorithm for sharded JSONL.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.8 (export algorithm)
- Refines bd-3m5.20

**Design**
- Implement checkpoint export in src/git/checkpoint/export.rs
- Materialize converged state for included namespaces
- Incremental export: track dirty shards via ApplyOutcome, regenerate only dirty
- Determinism: shard JSONL lines sorted by key
- Write manifest.json and meta.json with hashes

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Export writes shard files in sorted order
- [ ] Incremental: only dirty shards regenerated
- [ ] manifest.json lists files with hashes
- [ ] meta.json includes watermarks and content_hash

**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/export.rs

title: Phase 2: EventBody canonical CBOR + hashing
description:
**Problem**
Realtime WAL and replication require a canonical EventBody representation whose bytes are the hash preimage. There is no EventBody type or canonical encoding path in core yet, so we cannot guarantee stable hashing or verify remote frames.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.6 (canonical CBOR + hash over EventBody bytes), 2.4 (EventBody fields), 9.4 (EVENTS frame)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (EventBytes, EventFrameV1, VerifiedEvent)

**Files:** src/core/event.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (minicbor), tests under src/core
design:
**Design**
- Define EventBody, EventKindV1, and EventId in src/core/event.rs with StoreIdentity and NamespaceId baked in.
- Add EventBytes<Canonical> and EventBytes<Opaque> wrappers to separate locally authored canonical bytes from opaque remote bytes.
- Implement canonical CBOR encoder for EventBody using minicbor with definite lengths and sorted map keys for hashed types; reject floats and indefinite lengths.
- Provide helpers: encode_event_body_canonical -> EventBytes<Canonical>, hash_event_body -> Sha256.
- Add decode with strict bounds (depth, map entries, byte length) and return EventBytes<Opaque> + EventBody.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canonical encode produces stable bytes and hashes across runs (unit tests).
- [ ] Decoder rejects indefinite length and out of bounds payloads.
- [ ] EventBytes typestate is used in frame or WAL APIs to prevent hash preimage ambiguity.

title: Phase 6: Checkpoint local cache with atomic publish
description:
**Problem**
bd-3m5.20 is too large. Split: local cache for fast startup.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.8 step 8 (cache publish)
- Refines bd-3m5.20

**Design**
- Store verified copy under store_dir/checkpoint_cache/<group>/<checkpoint_id>/
- Atomic publish: write to .tmp/, fsync, rename, update CURRENT
- Keep last N checkpoints per group (default N=3), prune older
- CURRENT file points to newest verified checkpoint_id

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Cache written atomically with tmp+rename
- [ ] CURRENT updated after successful publish
- [ ] Pruning keeps only N most recent
- [ ] Startup reads from cache if available

**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/cache.rs

title: Phase 4: IPC namespace + durability + client_request_id fields
description:
**Problem**
IPC currently has no namespace, durability class, or client_request_id fields, and defaults are implemented ad hoc in CLI/daemon. This makes retries and read-your-writes semantics ambiguous and invites mismatched defaults across entry points.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC evolution) and §16.1 (request fields and defaults)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (NamespaceId, DurabilityClass, Seq0/Seq1)

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, src/daemon/query.rs
design:
**Design**
- Extend mutation request structs with optional fields: namespace, durability, client_request_id, actor_id, preserving backward compatibility.
- Parse namespace into NamespaceId and client_request_id into ClientRequestId early, so validation happens before mutation planning.
- Centralize defaulting rules in one place (e.g., Request::normalize): namespace defaults to core, durability defaults to LocalFsync, actor_id defaults to daemon actor.
- Add read/query gating fields require_min_seen and wait_timeout_ms; require_min_seen uses applied watermarks and returns retryable timeout error on miss.
- Ensure IPC JSON schema stays stable: new fields are optional and omitted in serialization when None.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Mutation request structs add optional namespace/durability/client_request_id/actor_id with backward-compatible defaults.
- [ ] Read/query request structs add optional require_min_seen + wait_timeout_ms with consistent defaulting.

title: Phase 1: DurabilityReceipt response types
description:
**Problem**
Receipts are the durable contract for retries and read-your-writes. Today there is no shared receipt type, so IPC responses risk being inconsistent and partial, and errors cannot safely return a receipt for retry.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11 (idempotency mapping + receipts), §10.5 (receipt semantics), §16.1 (IPC response shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (DurabilityProofV1, DurabilityOutcome)

**Files:** src/core/durability.rs, src/api/mod.rs

**Design**
- Define DurabilityProofV1 to capture the local fsync proof and optional replication acks.
- Define DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) to avoid ambiguous Option<DurabilityClass> fields.
- Define DurabilityReceipt { store: StoreIdentity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, min_seen } with serde support.
- Keep types serde-friendly for IPC JSON; daemon/IPC wiring lives in bd-3m5.14.
design:
**Design**
- Define DurabilityProofV1 to capture the local fsync proof and optional replication acks.
- Define DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) to avoid ambiguous Option<DurabilityClass> fields.
- Define DurabilityReceipt { store: StoreIdentity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, min_seen } with serde support.
- Ensure idempotent retry returns the same txn_id/event_ids, with monotonic improvement of durability_proof if new ACKs are observed.
- IPC success returns receipt; timeout returns retryable error with receipt included.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] DurabilityProofV1 and DurabilityOutcome types match REALTIME_PLAN.md semantics (local fsync + optional peer acks; pending vs achieved).
- [ ] DurabilityReceipt includes store identity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, and min_seen.
- [ ] Serde JSON roundtrip tests cover DurabilityReceipt and DurabilityProofV1.
- [ ] No daemon/IPC behavior changes in this bead (core types only).

title: Phase 2: core apply_event for EventBody
description:
**Problem**
The daemon mutates CanonicalState directly, so apply semantics are scattered and can diverge across mutation and replication paths. We need a single pure apply_event path that deterministically maps EventBody deltas to CRDT joins.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.8 (deterministic apply), 2.4 (TxnDeltaV1), 8.3 (apply pipeline)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (TxnOpV1, TxnDeltaV1)

**Files:** src/core/apply.rs (new), src/core/state.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/core/dep.rs, src/core/tombstone.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add core::apply_event(&mut CanonicalState, &EventBody) -> ApplyOutcome as a pure function with no I/O.
- Apply TxnDeltaV1 ops in canonical op key order so repeated application is deterministic.
- Use existing CRDT joins (Lww, Tombstone::join, DepEdge::join) and preserve tombstone resurrection rules.
- Detect note id collisions with mismatched content as corruption (return error), and treat duplicate note append as idempotent.
- Return ApplyOutcome with changed bead ids, dep keys, and note ids to drive indexing and checkpoint dirtiness.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apply_event is deterministic and idempotent on repeated events (tests).
- [ ] Note id collision with different content is detected and surfaced.
- [ ] ApplyOutcome reports changes used by indexing and checkpoint scheduling.

title: IPC: split malformed_payload vs invalid_request for JSON decode
description:
**Problem**
IPC JSON decode errors currently map to legacy parse_error, and semantic request validation failures get mislabeled as parse_error because decode_request_with_limits deserializes directly into Request. REALTIME_PLAN.md/REALTIME_ERRORS.md require malformed_payload for invalid JSON and invalid_request for valid JSON with semantic issues.

**Where**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: decode_request_with_limits, From<IpcError> for ErrorPayload

**Design**
- Separate JSON parsing from Request validation (e.g., parse to serde_json::Value first), then validate/deserialize with explicit error mapping.
- Map malformed JSON to code malformed_payload with details {parser:"json"...}, and semantic validation to invalid_request.
- Ensure payload shape uses realtime fields {code,message,retryable,retry_after_ms?,details?,receipt?}.

**Acceptance**
- Invalid JSON yields malformed_payload (not parse_error).
- Semantically invalid JSON yields invalid_request.
- IPC error payloads remain realtime-shaped and include details where applicable.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: Idempotency: detect client_request_id reuse mismatch
description:
**Problem**
The realtime code client_request_id_reuse_mismatch exists but cannot be emitted. WAL index upsert overwrites existing (namespace, origin_replica_id, client_request_id) rows, hiding mismatched reuse and mutating history.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode::ClientRequestIdReuseMismatch (defined)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError lacks a variant
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: upsert_client_request uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE

**Design**
- Add OpError variant and IPC mapping for client_request_id_reuse_mismatch with details {namespace, client_request_id, expected_request_sha256, got_request_sha256}.
- Change index behavior to preserve first-seen mapping; detect mismatch and return explicit error instead of overwriting.
- Keep idempotent reuse path returning original txn/event ids (no mutation) when request_sha matches.

**Acceptance**
- Reusing client_request_id with different request_sha256 returns client_request_id_reuse_mismatch (retryable=false).
- WAL index no longer overwrites request_sha256/txn_id/event_ids on conflict.
- Matching reuse returns prior ids without mutating history.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/core/error.rs

title: Idempotent retries reuse WAL index mapping
description:
**Problem**
Mutation planning generates a fresh txn_id per retry via the current write stamp. There is no visible fast-path to reuse existing txn/event ids for repeated client_request_id, which breaks idempotent retry semantics in REALTIME_PLAN.md.

**Where**
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs: MutationEngine::plan uses txn_id_for_stamp(&store, &write_stamp)
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: WalIndexReader::lookup_client_request exists but is not used in planning path

**Design**
- Before planning a mutation, lookup client_request_id in the WAL index.
- If found with matching request_sha256, return a plan that reuses the stored txn_id/event_ids and short-circuits event creation.
- If not found, proceed with normal planning and insert mapping.

**Acceptance**
- Retrying the same request returns the same txn/event ids (idempotent).
- No duplicate txn rows for repeated client_request_id.
- Coverage in Phase 4 idempotency tests (bd-3m5.60) updated.

**Files**
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs

title: Phase 3: Store dir layout + store lock
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement store directory layout and a store global lock. Current paths only expose a base data dir and WAL uses per remote snapshot filenames. Realtime needs `$BD_DATA_DIR/stores/<store_id>/` with a lock file and secure permissions.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.2 (process level locking), §15.1 (store layout), §7.3 (startup flow steps 2-4)
**Files:** src/paths.rs, src/daemon/store_lock.rs (new), src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (new) or src/daemon/core.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add path helpers in `src/paths.rs`: stores_dir, store_dir(store_id), store_meta_path, wal_dir, index_path, checkpoint_cache_dir.
- Implement a store lock file in the store dir with metadata: store_id, replica_id, pid, started_at_ms, daemon_version, last_heartbeat_ms.
- Acquire lock at store open; on failure show metadata and fail fast. No auto delete.
- Enforce file permissions (0700 for dirs, 0600 for sensitive files) and reject symlinks for critical paths.
- Expose a helper API for lock acquire and release (called by StoreRuntime open).
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] Lock file is created with required metadata and permissions; failure surfaces metadata.

- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

title: WAL replay: map wal_format_unsupported and header decode vs mismatch
description:
**Problem**
Replay maps all SegmentHeader errors to segment_header_mismatch, including unsupported wal_format_version and header decode failures. REALTIME_ERRORS.md distinguishes wal_format_unsupported from semantic header mismatch and from corruption.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs: SegmentHeader::decode reports unsupported wal format version
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_replay_error_code maps SegmentHeader to segment_header_mismatch
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: mirrored mapping

**Design**
- Add a specific error variant (or reason parsing) to detect wal_format_version mismatch and map to wal_format_unsupported with details {wal_format_version, supported}.
- Split decode failures (bad magic/CRC/truncated header) from semantic mismatch and map to wal_corrupt (or specific corruption code) instead of segment_header_mismatch.

**Acceptance**
- Unsupported wal_format_version yields wal_format_unsupported.
- Header decode failures yield corruption code, not segment_header_mismatch.
- True store_id/store_epoch mismatch continues to use segment_header_mismatch.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: WAL replay: verify payload sha256 (hash_mismatch)
description:
**Problem**
Replay does not verify sha256(payload_bytes) vs the record header, so hash_mismatch is effectively unreachable. CRC alone does not catch a writer that stored an incorrect sha.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: scan_segment decodes record payload but does not hash raw bytes
- src/daemon/wal/record.rs: RecordHeaderMismatch lacks a sha mismatch variant

**Design**
- Preserve raw payload bytes and compare sha256(payload_bytes) to record.header.event_sha256.
- Introduce a RecordHeaderMismatch variant (or WalReplayError) for hash mismatch and map to ErrorCode::HashMismatch with details.

**Acceptance**
- Replay detects sha mismatch and emits hash_mismatch with details.
- CRC-only mismatches continue to map to wal_corrupt.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs
- src/daemon/wal/record.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: WAL index: detect equivocation on EventId reuse
description:
**Problem**
Inserting an event with the same (namespace, origin_replica_id, origin_seq) but different sha triggers a SQLite constraint error and is mapped to index_corrupt, leaving equivocation unreachable.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: SqliteWalIndexTxn::record_event inserts into events with PK on (namespace, origin_replica_id, origin_seq)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_index_error_code maps WalIndexError::Sqlite(_) to index_corrupt

**Design**
- On conflict, detect existing row and compare sha256; if different, emit equivocation with details (namespace, origin_replica_id, origin_seq, expected_sha256, got_sha256).
- Avoid using generic SQLite error for equivocation.

**Acceptance**
- Equivocation is reported via ErrorCode::Equivocation, not index_corrupt.
- Normal duplicate insert with same sha remains idempotent (no error).

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: WAL replay: surface wal_tail_truncated repairs
description:

**Where**

- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs: open(...) discards ReplayStats

**Design**
- Emit wal_tail_truncated (retryable=true) when tail repair occurs, with details {namespace, segment, offset}.
- Surface ReplayStats to callers (StoreRuntime::open) so IPC can report the repair.

**Acceptance**
- Tail truncation emits a realtime error payload with details.
- StoreRuntime open path exposes replay stats for operator visibility.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: IPC error payloads: include details for WAL/index + lock permission_denied
description:
**Problem**
store_runtime_error_payload drops details for WAL index/replay errors, and lock permission_denied omits path/operation details. REALTIME_ERRORS.md expects structured details for machine handling.

**Where**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: store_runtime_error_payload (WalIndex/WalReplay)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: store_lock_error_payload (PermissionDenied without details)

**Design**
- Extend error payload mapping to include details for WAL index/replay errors (e.g., store_epoch_mismatch, prev_sha_mismatch, index_corrupt).
- Include permission_denied details for lock IO ({path, operation}).

**Acceptance**
- WAL/index error payloads include structured details per registry.
- Permission denied on lock IO includes path and operation.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/core/error.rs (if new details types needed)

title: Retryable flags: align lock_held and index_* with registry
description:
**Problem**
retryable flags do not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md: lock_held is marked retryable but should be false; index_corrupt and index_rebuild_required are treated as permanent but should be retryable after rebuild.

**Where**
- src/daemon/ops.rs: store_runtime_transience (lock_held)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_index_transience (index_corrupt/index_rebuild_required)

**Design**
- Update transience mapping to reflect registry retryable semantics.
- Ensure IPC ErrorPayload.retryable mirrors updated transience values.

**Acceptance**
- lock_held -> retryable=false
- index_corrupt/index_rebuild_required -> retryable=true

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: Admission/backpressure: emit overloaded/rate_limited/maintenance_mode
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_ERRORS.md defines overloaded, rate_limited, and maintenance_mode, but OpError has no variants and IPC cannot emit these codes. Limits exist but no visible error signaling.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode::{Overloaded,RateLimited,MaintenanceMode}
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError missing variants
- src/core/limits.rs: admission/backpressure limits

**Design**
- Add OpError variants and IPC mappings for overloaded/rate_limited/maintenance_mode with retry_after_ms when available.
- Hook AdmissionController/maintenance mode checks to return these errors on request admission.

**Acceptance**
- Overload and maintenance mode conditions emit the corresponding realtime error codes.
- retry_after_ms is set when known.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/* admission path (as implemented in bd-3m5.29)

title: Namespace policy errors: emit namespace_policy_violation/cross_namespace_dependency
description:
**Problem**
ErrorCode::{NamespacePolicyViolation,CrossNamespaceDependency} exist but have no OpError variants or IPC emission path, so namespace policy enforcement cannot surface structured errors.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode definitions
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError missing variants

**Design**
- Add OpError variants and IPC mappings with structured details per registry.
- Emit these codes from policy enforcement in replication/IPC admission once policies are loaded.

**Acceptance**
- Policy violations produce namespace_policy_violation or cross_namespace_dependency with details.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/* policy enforcement paths (post bd-3m5.17, bd-3m5.35)

title: Replication ingress: map DecodeError to realtime codes
description:
**Problem**
DecodeError is rich (non_canonical, decode limits, trailing bytes), but replay collapses decode failures into wal_corrupt and there is no visible mapping for replication error frames. REALTIME_ERRORS.md expects actionable codes (invalid_request, frame_too_large, batch_too_large, non_canonical).

**Where**
- src/core/event.rs: DecodeError
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: decode errors -> wal_corrupt
- replication ingress path (Phase 5)

**Design**
- Add DecodeError -> ErrorPayload mapping for replication/IPC ingress.
- Use non_canonical for canonical CBOR violations; size-related errors map to frame_too_large/batch_too_large; other semantic decode errors -> invalid_request.

**Acceptance**
- Replication ERROR frames include realtime codes for decode limits/non_canonical.
- Non-canonical CBOR is not reported as wal_corrupt in network paths.

**Files**
- src/core/event.rs
- src/daemon/* replication ingress code (bd-3m5.17/48)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs (if IPC shares mapping)

title: CRITICAL: disable fuzz-like proptest by default
description:
**Problem**
Default `cargo test` is effectively running fuzz-like property tests. In `src/git/sync.rs`, multiple `proptest!` blocks use the default `ProptestConfig` (256 cases) while each case initializes git repos, writes commits, pushes/fetches, and does ref resolution. This is expensive and makes the default test run feel like fuzzing.

Evidence:
- `src/git/sync.rs` has `proptest!` blocks without a custom `ProptestConfig`, so they run 256 cases each (e.g., around lines ~1600, ~1760, ~1900, ~2050).
- Each case spins up temp git repos and performs multiple git operations (commit, push, fetch), which is far heavier than unit tests.

**Design**
- Explicitly gate heavy proptests behind an opt-in mechanism:
  - Option A (preferred): `#[cfg(feature = "slow-tests")]` around the heavy proptest modules.
  - Option B: keep them enabled but set `ProptestConfig { cases: 8 }` by default and allow override via env (e.g., `BD_PROPTEST_CASES` or `PROPTEST_CASES`).
- Add a separate CI job or `just test-slow` to run the full proptest coverage (256+ cases) explicitly.
- Document in `docs/philosophy/test_design.md` or README that proptest-heavy suites are opt-in.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Default `cargo test` no longer runs the heavyweight proptest loops at full case count.
- [ ] A documented opt-in path exists to run the full proptest fuzz-like coverage.
- [ ] CI keeps at least one job that exercises full proptest coverage (or nightly).

**Files:**
- src/git/sync.rs
- justfile (optional: add test-slow target)
- docs/philosophy/test_design.md (optional)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (optional: separate slow job)

title: Phase 3: StoreRuntime + StoreId identity mapping
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: introduce StoreRuntime and move daemon identity from RemoteUrl to StoreId (hybrid mapping). Today `Daemon` is keyed by `RemoteUrl` and uses `path_to_remote` in `src/daemon/core.rs`, which blocks per store WAL and replication.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1 (StoreId as correctness identity) and §7.1-7.4 (StoreRuntime + discovery order)
- Migration fallback UUIDv5(normalized_remote_url) in §0.16
**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/remote.rs, src/daemon/repo.rs, src/paths.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `StoreRuntime` struct (initially minimal fields: meta, policies, state, watermarks, wal handle) in `src/daemon/store_runtime.rs`.
- Change `Daemon` maps to `BTreeMap<StoreId, StoreRuntime>` plus `path_to_store_id` and `remote_to_store_id` caches.
- Implement store identity discovery order: local store meta.json, refs/beads/meta store_meta.json, refs/beads/<store_id> listing, UUIDv5(normalized_remote_url) fallback for migration only.
- Keep RemoteUrl mapping for legacy git sync, but do not key correctness on it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Daemon state keyed by StoreId; remote URL only used for legacy git lane mapping.
- [ ] Store discovery order matches plan and is covered by tests or structured logs.
- [ ] Existing commands still resolve a store from repo path (compat) without behavior regression.

title: CRITICAL: tests are slow due to heavy CLI integration suite
description:
**Problem**
The test suite runtime is dominated by CLI integration tests that repeatedly spawn the `bd` binary, initialize git repos, and auto-start the daemon. `tests/critical_path.rs` alone has 83 tests, each doing multiple `bd` invocations (init/create/show/list/close/etc). `tests/migration.rs` adds 12 more, and `tests/daemon_lifecycle.rs` includes multi-second waits. This produces long wall-clock time and amplifies flakiness when tests run in parallel.

Evidence:
- `tests/critical_path.rs`: 83 `#[test]` cases, each building temp repos and running the CLI several times.
- `tests/migration.rs`: 12 integration tests with CLI + git usage.
- `tests/daemon_lifecycle.rs`: multiple sleeps (up to 5s) while waiting for shutdowns.

**Design**
- Add a fast default test tier:
  - Convert the critical-path file into fewer table-driven tests (one daemon + repo per test, multiple assertions per run).
  - Keep a smaller smoke subset enabled by default; move exhaustive CLI scenarios under `#[ignore]` or `feature = "slow-tests"`.
- Reuse a single daemon+repo fixture per test module to reduce repeated daemon startups.
- Add a `just test-fast` target for default runs and a `just test-slow` target for full integration coverage.
- Document the tiers and expected runtime budget.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Default `cargo test` runtime is substantially reduced (baseline recorded in bead; target under 2–3 min on dev machine).
- [ ] Full integration coverage remains available behind `--features slow-tests` or `cargo test -- --ignored`.
- [ ] The CLI critical-path coverage is preserved via table-driven tests or a dedicated slow suite.

**Files:**
- tests/critical_path.rs
- tests/migration.rs
- tests/daemon_lifecycle.rs
- justfile
- docs/philosophy/test_design.md (optional)

title: Namespaces still hard-coded to core; StoreState not wired
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.3/§2.7 require StoreState (namespaced CanonicalState) + namespace-aware access. Current implementation remains single-namespace: RepoState holds CanonicalState, and IPC normalization rejects any namespace other than core. This means namespaces.toml policies are loaded but never used, and non-core namespaces cannot be created or queried.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/core.rs:1865 normalize_namespace() returns NamespaceUnknown for any non-core.
- src/daemon/repo.rs: RepoState.state is CanonicalState (not StoreState).
- src/daemon/executor.rs and src/daemon/query_executor.rs apply/query against repo_state.state directly.

**Why this violates plan**
REALTIME_PLAN mandates StoreState = map<NamespaceId, CanonicalState> and a defaulting rule (namespace=core) rather than hard-rejecting non-core. This is a core architectural requirement.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Replace RepoState.state with StoreState; thread namespace selection through executor, query, replication ingest, checkpoint export/import.
- [ ] normalize_namespace validates against store.policies (namespaces.toml) and allows non-core namespaces when configured.
- [ ] Defaulting rule remains: missing namespace -> core.
- [ ] Unit tests cover non-core namespace mutation + query + replication paths.

title: Event WAL uses SegmentWriter per append; no persistent EventWal/segment reuse
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §5.5/§5.6 define an EventWal that owns the active segment per namespace and rotates by size/age. Current code opens a new SegmentWriter for every local mutation and every replication ingest batch, so each event/batch becomes its own segment and rotation logic is effectively bypassed. This contradicts the WAL design and inflates segment count + fsync dir overhead.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/executor.rs:225–255 creates SegmentWriter::open for each mutation (no reuse).
- src/daemon/core.rs:1009–1033 (ingest_remote_batch) creates SegmentWriter::open per batch.
- There is no EventWal struct managing active segments in src/daemon/wal/mod.rs.

**Why this violates plan**
Plan requires a long-lived EventWal that appends to the current segment, rotates when size/age thresholds are hit, and only fsyncs directories on rotation. Opening a new segment per event defeats these invariants.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Introduce EventWal (per store) that keeps active SegmentWriter per namespace.
- [ ] Use EventWal::append in executor + replication ingest; reuse active segment until rotation triggers.
- [ ] Rotation honors max_segment_bytes/max_segment_age and only then creates new segment + dir fsync.
- [ ] Tests cover multi-append into a single segment and rotation thresholds.

title: WAL segment sealing not recorded on rotation
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §5.2.3/§5.2.3 require marking rotated segments as sealed with final_len recorded in wal.sqlite. Current runtime writes segments with sealed=false and never updates them when rotation happens (AppendOutcome.rotated is unused). Sealing only occurs during replay rebuild, so live index misses sealed/final_len invariants.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/executor.rs:264–282 upsert_segment always uses sealed=false, final_len=None.
- src/daemon/core.rs:1041–1066 does the same for replication ingest.
- SegmentWriter::append returns AppendOutcome.rotated, but no caller updates sealed/final_len.

**Why this violates plan**
Plan mandates sealed segments be recorded at rotation time and validated on startup (file_len == final_len). Without this, on-disk corruption and partial writes can go undetected.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] On segment rotation, mark previous segment sealed=1 and final_len in wal.sqlite.
- [ ] Ensure index updates are atomic with segment rotation.
- [ ] Admin/status and replay enforce sealed invariants for live-written segments, not just rebuild.

title: EventBody decode rejects unknown keys; breaks forward-compat storage/forwarding
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.6/§3.4 says nodes can store/forward raw EventBody bytes without re-encoding and should be forward compatible. Current decode_event_body_map / decode_hlc_max hard-fail on any unknown key, which means a newer sender adding a field causes older nodes to reject the event entirely (even though hash is over raw bytes).

Evidence:
- src/core/event.rs:478–510 decode_event_body_map returns InvalidField for unknown key.
- src/core/event.rs:1618–1644 decode_hlc_max returns InvalidField for unknown key.

**Why this violates plan**
Forward compatibility is required in v0.5: receivers should accept unknown keys (after hash verification) and still persist raw bytes, even if they can’t interpret new fields.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Unknown keys in EventBody/HlcMax are skipped (while enforcing size/depth limits).
- [ ] Events with extra fields still verify sha256 and are persisted/replicated.
- [ ] Tests cover decoding EventBody with an extra field without failure.

title: CBOR decode does not reject non-canonical integer encodings
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §3.1 requires rejecting non-canonical integer encodings in EventBody. Current decode uses minicbor::Decoder u64/u32 directly and never checks for non-minimal integer encodings, so overlong integer encodings are accepted.

Evidence:
- src/core/event.rs:1650–1667 decode_u32 uses dec.u64 with no canonicality check.
- No canonical integer validation exists in decode_map_len/decode_array_len/decode_u32.

**Why this violates plan**
Canonical CBOR is a correctness primitive for hashed payloads. Accepting non-canonical integers undermines deterministic validation requirements.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Implement strict integer decoding that rejects non-minimal encodings (and tags).
- [ ] Apply to all integer fields in EventBody/TxnDelta/HlcMax.
- [ ] Add tests that reject overlong integer encodings.

title: Replica liveness table unused; last_seen_ms not persisted
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §2.1.2 calls for persisting last_seen_ms for known replicas so admin.status and pruning remain correct across restarts. Schema includes replica_liveness, but it is never written or read.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs defines replica_liveness table, but there are no writer/read methods for it.
- rg "replica_liveness" only finds the schema.

**Why this violates plan**
Without persistence, replica liveness resets on restart, breaking visibility of peers and any future pruning logic.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Add WAL index writer/read APIs for replica_liveness.
- [ ] Update liveness on replication handshake/ACKs with last_seen_ms.
- [ ] Surface persisted liveness in admin.status.

title: Store lock heartbeat never updated
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.2 recommends updating store.lock last_heartbeat_ms periodically while holding the lock. StoreLock::update_heartbeat exists but is never called, so lock metadata becomes stale immediately.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/store_lock.rs defines update_heartbeat, but no call sites (rg update_heartbeat).

**Why this violates plan**
Heartbeat is the intended staleness signal for operators. Without updates, bd store unlock cannot reliably distinguish live vs stale locks.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Add periodic heartbeat updates (e.g., in daemon run loop every ~10s).
- [ ] Heartbeat failures are logged but do not crash daemon.

title: Config additions missing for replication/checkpoint/namespace defaults
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §15.2 requires config for checkpoint groups, namespace defaults, and replication settings. Current config only includes auto_upgrade + limits; replication uses env vars and checkpoint groups are hard-coded to core.

Evidence:
- src/config.rs: Config has only auto_upgrade + limits.
- src/daemon/core.rs uses BD_REPL_LISTEN_ADDR / BD_REPL_MAX_CONNECTIONS env vars.
- src/daemon/checkpoint_scheduler.rs uses core_default only; no config-backed groups.

**Why this violates plan**
The plan explicitly calls out config-driven checkpoint groups and replication parameters. Missing config blocks real deployments and policy-driven behavior.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Extend Config to include replication listen addr/peers/timeouts and checkpoint group definitions.
- [ ] Load namespace policy defaults from config or namespaces.toml as specified.
- [ ] Replace env-only replication settings with config precedence (env can override).
- [ ] Tests cover config roundtrip + defaulting.

title: WAL append uses sync_data instead of full fsync
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.10 defines LocalFsync as fsync of the active segment file after each append. Current SegmentWriter::append uses File::sync_data, which may omit metadata durability (file length/mtime) on some platforms.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs:252–260 calls self.file.sync_data().

**Why this violates plan**
LocalFsync semantics require full fsync of the file to guarantee record durability.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Use sync_all (fsync) for LocalFsync record durability.
- [ ] Document/benchmark any performance impact and keep directory fsync only on rotation.

title: Phase 3: Event WAL framing + segment writer
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement the event WAL segment format and framing. Current `src/daemon/wal.rs` is a snapshot WAL; there is no event log, framing, or segment rotation.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.9 (segment naming), §0.10 (LocalFsync), §6.4 and §6.5 (crash consistency)
- Framing uses crc32c (REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1)
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/mod.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/segment.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/record.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Define WAL segment header with store_id, store_epoch, namespace, wal_format_version, created_at_ms, segment_id.
- Define record header with EventId fields, sha256, prev_sha256 (optional), event_time_ms, txn_id, client_request_id, request_sha256 (optional), and record_len.
- Implement frame encoding with length + crc32c and tail truncation handling.
- Implement segment rotation by size and age; create files under wal/<namespace>/segment-<created_at_ms>-<segment_id>.wal.
- Implement LocalFsync semantics: fsync segment after append; fsync directory only on new segment creation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Can append and read back a record frame with crc32c validation.
- [ ] Segment creation and rotation follow naming and fsync rules.
- [ ] MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES enforced before writing.

title: EventBody decoder doesn't detect duplicate map keys
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §3.1 says duplicate map keys in EventBody SHOULD be rejected. Current decode_event_body_map/other decoders accept duplicates silently (later key wins).

Evidence:
- src/core/event.rs decode_event_body_map loops over keys with no duplicate tracking.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Track seen keys in EventBody/TxnDelta/HlcMax and return DecodeError::DuplicateKey (or similar) on duplicates.
- [ ] Add tests for duplicate keys being rejected.

title: Default namespaces only include core; sys/wf/tmp defaults unused
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §2.2 lists default policies for core/sys/wf/tmp. StoreRuntime::load_namespace_policies falls back to default_policies() which only inserts core, so sys/wf/tmp are never present unless explicitly configured.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs:335–341 default_policies inserts only NamespaceId::core().
- src/core/namespace.rs defines sys_default/wf_default/tmp_default but they are unused.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] When namespaces.toml is missing, populate defaults for core/sys/wf/tmp per plan.
- [ ] Add tests asserting default policy map includes sys/wf/tmp.

title: Startup does not clean up orphaned *.wal.tmp segments
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §5.2.3 says *.wal.tmp files MUST be ignored and SHOULD be deleted if not referenced by wal.sqlite. Current replay ignores .tmp files but never cleans them up.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs list_segments only loads *.wal; no cleanup of *.wal.tmp.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] On startup/replay, delete .wal.tmp files not referenced in wal.sqlite segments table.
- [ ] Log deletions for operator visibility.

title: HLC state is global; per-actor persistence not honored
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §8.4 requires HLC state to be monotonic per ActorId and persisted/restored per actor. Current daemon uses a single global Clock and only restores HLC state for the daemon actor, so custom actor_id requests can regress after restart.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/clock.rs implements a single Clock without actor partitioning.
- src/daemon/core.rs:564–570 loads hlc_state only for self.actor.
- MutationEngine uses Clock::tick for all actors (src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs:200–240).

**Why this violates plan**
Per-actor monotonicity and persistence are required; using a single clock and only restoring the daemon actor breaks this for alternate actor_id inputs.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Maintain per-actor HLC state (e.g., map ActorId -> Clock state) and load from wal_index.hlcs for any actor seen/used.
- [ ] When actor_id is specified in a request, advance the correct actor clock state.
- [ ] Tests cover monotonicity across restarts for non-daemon actor_id.

title: I/O budgeting limits (bytes/sec) are defined but not enforced
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.19 requires enforcing MAX_REPL_INGEST_BYTES_PER_SEC and MAX_BACKGROUND_IO_BYTES_PER_SEC (token buckets) so WAL writes aren’t starved. Limits exist but are unused.

Evidence:
- src/core/limits.rs defines max_repl_ingest_bytes_per_sec / max_background_io_bytes_per_sec.
- No call sites reference these fields (rg shows only definitions/tests).

**Why this violates plan**
These budgets are normative; without enforcement, replication or checkpoints can saturate IO and violate the durability priority rule.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Implement token-bucket throttling for replication ingest bytes/sec and background IO.
- [ ] Ensure WAL append + fsync are not starved by background work.
- [ ] Add metrics/tests verifying throttling behavior.

title: Checkpoint job queue limit is defined but never enforced
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.19 requires MAX_CHECKPOINT_JOB_QUEUE enforcement (coalesce by group when full). Limits include max_checkpoint_job_queue, but scheduler never consults it.

Evidence:
- src/core/limits.rs defines max_checkpoint_job_queue.
- No call sites reference it (rg shows only definitions/tests).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Enforce max_checkpoint_job_queue in checkpoint scheduler (per store), coalescing when full.
- [ ] Add tests verifying queue limit behavior.

title: Checkpoint import not wired into daemon load path
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §13 expects Git lane to use checkpoint import/export for bootstrap and recovery. Current daemon load path still reads only legacy refs/heads/beads/store; checkpoint import code exists but is never invoked. This means checkpoint lane is export-only and cannot be used for bootstrap/catch-up.

Evidence:
- src/daemon/git_worker.rs: load()/load_local() read refs/heads/beads/store via git::sync.
- rg import_checkpoint shows no call sites outside tests.

**Why this violates plan**
Plan requires checkpoint import to drive bootstrap and recovery in v0.5 (snapshot bootstrap is deferred, but checkpoints are not).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Wire checkpoint import into daemon load path (alongside legacy lane for migration).
- [ ] Ensure imported checkpoints set applied/durable watermarks to included values.
- [ ] Add tests for loading from checkpoint ref.

title: KEEPALIVE_MS limit unused; no keepalive scheduling
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §0.19 defines KEEPALIVE_MS (5s) for protocol keepalive, but code never uses limits.keepalive_ms. Replication sessions and IPC do not send keepalives or track idle timeouts based on this value.

Evidence:
- rg "keepalive_ms" only finds definitions in src/core/limits.rs.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Implement keepalive timers in replication sessions (HELLO/ACK pings) and/or IPC streaming.
- [ ] Wire to Limits.keepalive_ms with tests for idle connections.

title: CLI namespace/durability/client-request-id flags become config-backed overrides
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN §16.3 adds CLI flags `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id`. We want these to remain, but semantics should be config‑first: repo/user config provides defaults and flags override those defaults. Env vars must remain supported and take precedence over config (but not over explicit CLI flags).

**Design**
- Keep existing CLI flags.
- Resolve defaults from layered config (repo `beads.toml` + user config) with env overrides.
- Precedence: CLI flag > env var > repo config > user config > hardcoded defaults.
- `--client-request-id` remains explicit‑only (no default); used mainly by automation.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] CLI behavior matches precedence above for namespace/durability defaults.
- [ ] Env vars for replication/config remain supported.
- [ ] CLI flags continue to pass through IPC unchanged.
- [ ] Tests cover: config default used when no flags; CLI overrides config; env overrides config.

title: Layered config (user+repo beads.toml) with central module
description:
**Problem**
Config is currently minimal (`src/config.rs` only has auto_upgrade + limits). REALTIME_PLAN §15.2 requires replication/checkpoint/namespace defaults, and the project needs a maintainable, central config system. We also want repo‑level `beads.toml` (plus user config) with clear precedence and env overrides.

**Design**
- Introduce a dedicated config module (e.g., `src/config/mod.rs` with submodules `schema.rs`, `load.rs`, `merge.rs`).
- Define layered config types:
  - `UserConfig` (global defaults, stored at `~/.config/beads-rs/config.toml`).
  - `RepoConfig` (repo‑level `beads.toml`).
  - `EffectiveConfig` (merged result used by CLI/daemon).
- Keep store‑local files (`namespaces.toml`, `replicas.toml`, store meta) as authoritative for store identity/policy, but allow repo config to supply defaults when those files are absent.
- Precedence: env vars > repo config > user config > defaults.
- Provide helper to locate repo root and load `beads.toml`.

**Config schema sketch**
- `[defaults]` (namespace, durability, actor?)
- `[replication]` (listen_addr, max_connections, peers, timeouts)
- `[checkpoint.groups.<name>]` (namespaces, git_ref, writers, debounce/max_interval/max_events, durable_copy_via_git)
- `[namespaces.<name>]` (NamespacePolicy defaults if store config missing)
- `[limits]` (existing Limits override)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Central config module with clear layering/merge logic and tests.
- [ ] `beads.toml` supported at repo root; user config still supported.
- [ ] Env overrides are preserved.
- [ ] CLI + daemon use `EffectiveConfig` instead of ad‑hoc reads where applicable.

notes.content: Env var audit: map to config where appropriate. Recommend: BD_ACTOR -> defaults.actor (env remains override); BD_REPL_LISTEN_ADDR -> replication.listen_addr; BD_REPL_MAX_CONNECTIONS -> replication.max_connections; BD_LOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS -> timeouts.git_load_secs (or similar). Keep env-only: BD_REMOTE_URL, BD_STORE_ID (debug overrides); BD_DATA_DIR/BD_CONFIG_DIR/BD_WAL_DIR; LOG; BD_NO_AUTO_UPGRADE (override); BD_UPGRADE_* (test/upgrade harness); BD_TEST_WAL_HANG_* (slow-tests). BD_TOMBSTONE_TTL_MS is legacy tombstone GC for git sync; leave env-only or add to a legacy config section if we intentionally support it.
notes.author: darin@book

title: Add --type flag to bd update command
description:
**Problem**
Cannot change a beads type after creation. Had to close bd-d37 and recreate as epic bd-2fr.

**Solution**
Add `--type` flag to `bd update`:
```
bd update <id> --type=epic
```

**Design Notes**
- Should validate type is one of: task, bug, feature, epic, chore
- Type changes should be LWW like other fields
- Consider if changing TO epic should warn if bead has no children
- Consider if changing FROM epic should warn if bead has children

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd update --type <TYPE>` works
- [ ] Invalid types rejected with clear error
- [ ] Type change reflected in `bd show`

title: Tombstone GC policy can allow ID reuse/resurrection
description:
**Problem**
Tombstones are GC'd after 30 days in sync merge. If an ID is resurrected after GC, collisions can reappear or deleted IDs can be reused unintentionally.

**Design**
Revisit GC: either make tombstones permanent, use a much longer TTL, or store a compact ID-reuse guard (e.g., bloom filter or min hash) to prevent resurrection. Consider making GC configurable.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] GC policy documented and configurable or safe by default
- [ ] Regression test for resurrection after GC

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/core/state.rs, SPEC.md

title: cargo test fails in src/daemon/ipc.rs tests: missing BeadId import for Query::Show/Deps
Add command aliases: deps for dep, ls for list

Add common aliases for better discoverability:
- 'deps' and 'dependencies' as aliases for 'dep'
- 'ls' as alias for 'list'

title: Smart commit messages from beads context
description:
Make sync commit messages on refs/heads/beads/store more descriptive. Instead of 'sync: +1 created', show what actually changed:

- created bd-xxx: "Title here"
- updated bd-yyy desc: "new description"  
- closed bd-zzz: "reason"
- deleted bd-abc

This helps with debugging and auditing bead changes over time. Since these commits live on a separate ref (beads/store), they won't clutter the main code history.

title: Replication watermarks should use Seq0 typed values
description:
**Problem**
Replication protocol types (`WatermarkMap`, `Want`, `Ack`) use raw `u64` sequence numbers in `src/daemon/repl/proto.rs`. Multiple layers then convert these to `Seq0`/`Watermark`, which is easy to misuse and obscures invariants.

**Files**
- src/daemon/repl/proto.rs
- src/daemon/repl/session.rs
- src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs
- src/daemon/repl/runtime.rs
- src/daemon/repl/want.rs
design: Introduce a typed wire map (e.g., `WireSeq0` or `WireWatermarkMap`) that stores `Seq0` in memory and encodes/decodes as `u64` on the wire. Replace `WatermarkMap` aliases with the typed version across replication logic, and update CBOR encoding/decoding accordingly. This keeps all internal code on `Seq0`/`Watermark` while preserving the protocol format.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Replication protocol code uses `Seq0`-typed watermark values internally.
- [ ] Encode/decode preserves the existing on-wire format.
- [ ] All conversions from raw `u64` removed from session/peer_acks paths.

title: Add bd create --file for bulk creation from markdown
description:
beads-go supports creating beads from a markdown file:
  bd create --file issues.md --type feature

This enables batch creation workflows. Also consider --from-template support.

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/create.rs

title: Tests for namespace propagation in issue views
description: **Problem**\nIssue views now include namespace, but we lack a focused test to lock down propagation for Issue (not just IssueSummary).
design: Add a unit test in src/daemon/query_executor.rs that exercises Issue::from_bead via the query executor path and asserts Issue.namespace == read namespace. Use a non-core NamespaceId to ensure it's not defaulted.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Test asserts Issue.namespace equals read namespace\n- [ ] Existing helpers updated if needed

title: Spec-aligned WAL/event invariants and idempotency canonicalization
Tests: remove global env locks by avoiding env mutation
description:
**Problem**
Tests currently serialize via a global env lock because fixtures set process-wide env (BD_DATA_DIR/XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/BD_WAL_DIR). This blocks parallel execution and is fragile.

**Design**
- Avoid global env mutation in tests.
- For CLI integration tests, set env only on the Command (already done in some fixtures).
- For in-process IPC helpers (e.g., fixtures/load_gen using send_request), introduce explicit runtime/socket overrides instead of global env:
  - Add test-only overrides in daemon::ipc for socket dir (similar to paths::set_data_dir_for_tests), or new send_request_* APIs that accept a socket path/runtime dir.
  - Update fixtures to pass runtime_dir/socket path explicitly and remove std::env::set_var usage.
- Remove env locks once overrides are in place.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No test fixture calls std::env::set_var for BD_* or XDG_RUNTIME_DIR.
- [ ] Realtime/load_gen/IPC fixtures accept explicit runtime/socket paths.
- [ ] Tests run with --test-threads=8 without env-related flakiness.

**Files**
- tests/fixtures/realtime.rs
- tests/fixtures/store_dir.rs
- tests/fixtures/load_gen.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs (test overrides or explicit socket path APIs)
- src/paths.rs (if adding runtime dir override)

title: Daemon init should not ignore ensure_repo_loaded errors; dedupe SyncWait
description:
## What's Wrong
`Request::Init` ignores errors from `ensure_repo_loaded` (TODO in code). Also `SyncWait` behavior is duplicated: `Daemon::handle_request` returns Synced immediately, but `run_state_loop` has special-case barrier logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/core.rs:585 (TODO: ignores ensure_repo_loaded errors after init)
- src/daemon/core.rs:559 (SyncWait returns immediately)
- src/daemon/server.rs:45 (SyncWait handled as a barrier before calling daemon)

## Why It Matters
Init can report success even if repo state isn't loadable, leading to confusing follow-on failures. Duplicated SyncWait logic makes correctness brittle and creates drift.

## Suggested Fix
- Make Init return an error (or include warning details) if `ensure_repo_loaded` fails.
- Consolidate SyncWait into one place (prefer state loop barrier) and remove/clarify the other path.

## Acceptance
- `bd init` fails (or returns structured warning) when it cannot load the repo state.
- `SyncWait` has a single source of truth (no duplicate paths).

title: EventBody: move hlc_max+delta into EventKindV1::TxnV1
description:
**Problem**
`src/core/event.rs` defines `EventBody { delta: TxnDeltaV1, hlc_max: Option<HlcMax> }` and `EventKindV1::TxnV1` carries no data. The realtime plan + `beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs` expect `EventKindV1::TxnV1(TxnV1{hlc_max, delta})`. The optional `hlc_max` lets invalid events exist; the executor currently errors at runtime when it is missing ("event missing hlc_max"). Invariants are not encoded in the type system.

**Files**
- src/core/event.rs
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- src/daemon/executor.rs
- src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (tests)
- other event encode/decode callers
design:
Refactor the event model to match the sketch:
- Introduce `TxnV1 { hlc_max: HlcMax, delta: TxnDeltaV1 }`.
- Change `EventKindV1` to `TxnV1(TxnV1)`.
- Remove `EventBody.delta` and `EventBody.hlc_max`.
- Update canonical encode/decode to require HLC max for txn bodies; decide whether to accept legacy payloads for compatibility or reject them explicitly.
- Update apply/verification paths and tests to construct the new `EventKindV1::TxnV1` payload.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `EventBody` no longer has optional `hlc_max` or top-level `delta` fields.
- [ ] `TxnV1` always includes `hlc_max` and `delta`, and `EventKindV1::TxnV1` carries it.
- [ ] encode/decode enforces HLC presence; no runtime "event missing hlc_max" error.
- [ ] Updated event/repl tests pass.

title: Add daemon control commands (stop, restart, status)
description: Add subcommands to 'bd daemon' for controlling the daemon: stop, restart, status. Currently have to kill the process manually.

title: Git sync state-machine robustness tests
description:
**Problem**
We lack explicit tests for sync behavior under diverged refs, non-fast-forward pushes, and stale local refs. Regressions here can cause data loss or stuck syncs.

**Design**
Add tests that simulate:
- local ahead of remote (backup ref created, local not clobbered)
- remote ahead of local (fast-forward update succeeds)
- local/remote diverged (divergence flagged, sync outcome carries warning)
- push non-fast-forward retry loop respects max_retries
Use temp git repos with controlled commit graphs.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests cover the above scenarios
- [ ] Sync outcome divergence is surfaced
- [ ] No data loss (local commits preserved)

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, tests/git_sync.rs

title: Refactor send_request and send_request_no_autostart to share common code
description:
## What's Wrong
send_request and send_request_no_autostart in src/daemon/ipc.rs duplicate the request encoding/response parsing logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/ipc.rs:828-838 (send_request)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs:844-859 (send_request_no_autostart)

## Why It Matters
If we change the wire format or add error handling, we need to update both places.

## Suggested Fix
Extract common send/receive logic into a helper that takes a stream.

title: Integration tests isolate BD_DATA_DIR per runtime
description: Integration tests that spawn `bd` set runtime/WAL dirs but not BD_DATA_DIR. The daemon then uses the default store path and can collide with a developer daemon or other test daemons, causing lock_held failures.
design: Derive a temp data dir from each test runtime dir (e.g., runtime_dir/data) and set BD_DATA_DIR on every integration-test `bd` command (helpers + direct invocations). Ensure the directory exists before spawning commands.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All integration-test helpers and raw commands set BD_DATA_DIR per runtime
- [ ] daemon_lifecycle and critical_path tests no longer fail due to store lock conflicts
- [ ] cargo test passes (or targeted suites) after the change

title: Profile test suite with samply
description: Capture samply profile for cargo test and summarize hotpaths.
closed_reason: Captured samply-test-suite.json; top samples in nanosleep/semwait + bd process wait time; identified IPC ping + extra create round-trip + test data-dir lock as bottlenecks.

title: Replication pending_events queue is unbounded
description:
**Problem**
Both inbound and outbound replication loops buffer events before streaming is established, but the pending_events Vec has no size/byte bound. If handshake stalls or a peer stays non-streaming, this can grow without bound and consume memory.

**Evidence**
- src/daemon/repl/manager.rs: pending_events: Vec<BroadcastEvent> grows while !streaming.
- src/daemon/repl/server.rs: pending_events: Vec<BroadcastEvent> grows while !streaming.

**Why this hurts**
A slow or stuck peer can cause unbounded memory growth in the daemon, which is brittle and hard to reason about.
design:
**Design**
Option A: Replace pending_events with a bounded VecDeque that caps both event count and bytes (use limits.max_event_batch_events / max_event_batch_bytes or broadcaster hot-cache limits). Drop oldest events and record a metric when overflowing.
Option B: Remove pending_events entirely and rely on hot_cache + WANT after handshake, avoiding buffering while not streaming.

Either way, prevent unbounded growth and keep behavior deterministic.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] pending_events is bounded by size/bytes or removed entirely.
- [ ] Overflow behavior is explicit (drop policy + metric/log).
- [ ] Tests or assertions cover the cap behavior.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/manager.rs, src/daemon/repl/server.rs

title: Detect force-push and surface explicit warning in sync status
Add dependency cycle detection helper in CanonicalState
description:
**Problem**
Cycle detection logic lives inline in query validation and is not reusable for the CLI dep cycles command.

**Design**
Add a CanonicalState helper (e.g., dependency_cycles) that returns detected cycles over active deps (all kinds). Keep it deterministic by sorting inputs and returning stable paths.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] New cycle detection helper in src/core/state.rs (or src/core/dep.rs)
- [ ] Tests cover at least one cycle and one acyclic graph
- [ ] Helper returns deterministic ordering

title: Create op should return Issue to skip extra show
description:
**Problem**
CLI create does a mutation then immediately queries show to render JSON/human output. In integration tests this doubles IPC round-trips and shows up as wait time in profiling.

**Design**
Add an optional Issue payload on OpResponse. When the mutation result is Created, attach Issue::from_bead from the updated state. CLI should consume OpResponse.issue when present and skip the follow-up show; fall back to current behavior when missing.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] OpResponse serializes issue when created
- [ ] CLI create uses issue when present and avoids extra IPC query
- [ ] Existing JSON output shape remains (QueryResult::Issue)
- [ ] Tests updated/added to cover issue attachment

**Files**: src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/commands/create.rs
closed_reason: OpResponse now carries optional Issue for Created ops; daemon attaches issue from state, CLI reuses it to skip extra show; added executor test.

title: Validate WAL record header vs EventBody payload
description:
**Problem**
Record header fields (origin_replica_id, origin_seq, event_time_ms, txn_id, client_request_id) are not validated against decoded EventBody bytes during replay or fsck. Corrupted headers can silently diverge while sha matches payload. REALTIME_PLAN.md 5.2 requires header consistency checks.

**Design**
Decode EventBody from Record.payload using core::decode_event_body (limits) in replay and fsck; compare fields with RecordHeader. On mismatch:
- fsck: report corruption (new FsckEvidenceCode) and quarantine/repair per mode.
- replay: return explicit WalReplayError (non-tail corruption).
Add tests with a record whose payload is valid but header is tampered; assert fsck reports mismatch and replay errors.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Header/body mismatches are detected in replay and fsck.
- [ ] Fsck report includes deterministic evidence code.
- [ ] Tests cover header mismatch path; cargo test passes.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs, src/daemon/wal/record.rs, tests/phase3_fsck.rs

title: Remove just --list from bd prime; remove repo hook in favor of global
description:
Two cleanup items:
1. bd prime currently shows just --list output - remove this
2. The claude code hook in this repo should be removed; leave it configured globally instead

title: Document CLI read gating flags
description:
**Problem**
CLI_SPEC.md does not document the read gating flags required by REALTIME_PLAN, which makes the feature discoverability poor for realtime debugging.

title: Verify bd upgrade downloads with checksums/signatures

Upgrade downloads prebuilt binaries without integrity checks.

**Design**

**Acceptance**

title: Test bead for bv viewer integration
Add golden output test for bd store fsck
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN recommends golden corpus tests for `bd store fsck` output stability, but we currently have no CLI-level rendering tests. Output drift can silently break operator workflows.

**Design**
Add a unit test for render_fsck_human in src/cli/commands/store.rs using a hand-built FsckReport that includes repairs and evidence. Assert exact output to lock formatting.

**Files**

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test asserts exact human output for a representative FsckReport (repairs + evidence)
- [ ] No changes to fsck runtime logic

title: Daemon load blocks on SSH auth, hangs IPC
description:
**Problem**
First access to a repo blocks the daemon in `Daemon::ensure_repo_loaded` while waiting for `GitOp::Load` to finish. `GitWorker::load` always performs a remote fetch (even in best-effort mode). If SSH auth is misconfigured (no agent / passphrase prompt / stalled handshake), libgit2 fetch can hang. Because `ensure_repo_loaded` waits synchronously, the state thread blocks and IPC requests like `bd status` hang indefinitely. We saw `bd status` time out on this machine before SSH auth was configured.

**Design**
Make load non-blocking, and treat timeouts as a guardrail rather than the primary fix:
- Split `GitWorker::load` into a fast local-only load (read local ref, compute state) and a remote refresh path.
- Change `ensure_repo_loaded` to return local state immediately and, if the repo is stale, enqueue a background `GitOp::Refresh` (or `GitOp::Fetch`) without blocking.
- Only block on remote fetch when no local ref exists; add a bounded timeout (default ~30s, configurable) and return a clean, actionable error on timeout with diagnostic info (SSH agent/auth hints, remote URL).
- Surface fetch/auth errors via `fetch_error` and Sync warnings, but keep IPC responsive.

**Design Notes**
The primary goal is responsiveness: read-only queries must not block on network or auth. Use best-effort refresh for freshness, but never let it block IPC. Timeout is a secondary safety net for unavoidable fetches. Keep existing CRDT merge semantics intact.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd status` returns promptly (e.g., <1s) when SSH auth is missing/invalid; no hang.
- [ ] If local ref exists, initial load is non-blocking and refresh happens in the background.
- [ ] If no local ref exists, the initial fetch is bounded by a default ~30s timeout and returns a clean, actionable error on timeout with diagnostics.
- [ ] Fetch/auth failures appear as warnings (`fetch_error`) but do not block IPC responses.
- [ ] Background refresh still updates cached state once auth/network is available.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/core.rs`, `src/daemon/git_worker.rs`, `src/git/sync.rs`

title: Harden git sync state machine edge cases
description:
**Problem**
Sync/load/refresh pathways have overlapping but different logic. Edge cases (diverged refs, remote missing ref, local ahead, dirty during refresh, rewritten history) are not fully covered by tests. This risks subtle data loss or confusing behavior.

**Design**
Add a focused sync-state test harness that builds local/remote repos and exercises: missing refs, local-ahead, remote-ahead, diverged, force-push rewrite, fetch failures, dirty during refresh. Assert invariants: no local data loss, backups created, errors surfaced. Consider consolidating merge logic into a single path to reduce divergence.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests cover diverged/local-ahead/force-push scenarios.
- [ ] Failures are explicit; no silent state drops.
- [ ] Backups created when local history would be overwritten.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/git_worker.rs, tests/critical_path.rs

title: Add richer list filtering (date ranges, pattern matching)
description:
beads-go has comprehensive list filtering that beads-rs lacks:
- Date range filters: --created-after, --updated-after, --closed-after
- Pattern matching: --title-contains, --desc-contains
- Priority ranges: --priority-min, --priority-max (partially done)
- Empty/null checks: --no-assignee, --no-labels, --empty-description

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/list.rs

title: Clock time-source injection + monotonic tests
description:
**Problem**
Clock behavior is hard to test deterministically. We should be able to inject time sources to simulate skew and verify monotonicity across restarts.

**Design**
Introduce a TimeSource trait for Clock (SystemTimeSource default). Allow tests to inject a fake source. Add tests covering backward/forward jumps and ensure stamps are monotonic and skew is detected.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Clock supports injected time source in tests
- [ ] Monotonicity tests cover backward/forward jumps
- [ ] No API changes to production callers (Clock::new remains default)

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/daemon/core.rs

title: Replication ingest can stall on contiguous batches
description:
**Problem**
When an EVENTS message contains multiple contiguous events for the same (namespace, origin), only the first event is verified as contiguous. Subsequent events are verified with expected_prev=None and become Deferred, then buffered. The gap buffer never promotes deferred events after durable advances, so those events can remain stuck and trigger unnecessary WANTs even though we already received them.

**Evidence**
- src/daemon/repl/session.rs: handle_events() computes expected_prev via expected_prev_head() using the durable watermark snapshot (not updated per-frame).
- src/core/event.rs: verify_event_frame() returns VerifiedEventAny::Deferred when expected_prev_head is None for seq>1.
- src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs: ingest_one() buffers deferred events and advance_durable_batch() does not re-check buffered deferred events.

**Why this hurts**
A peer that legitimately batches seq N and N+1 in a single EVENTS frame will cause seq N+1 to be buffered and never applied unless it is resent later. This can stall replication and produce spurious WANTs.
design:
**Design**
Option A (preferred): Track per-(namespace, origin) last-seen head within the current EVENTS batch. Use that to compute expected_prev_head for later frames in the same batch so contiguous batches verify as Contiguous.

Option B: Extend GapBuffer to promote deferred events when durable advances (e.g., add a flush_ready() that re-validates deferred prev against the new durable head). Call it after advance_durable_batch().

Whichever approach, ensure events already in the batch are not re-requested via WANT.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A contiguous batch (seq 1,2) in a single EVENTS message applies both events and emits no WANT.
- [ ] Buffered deferred events are eventually forwarded once their prev is known (test covers).
- [ ] Add regression test in src/daemon/repl/session.rs and/or gap_buffer.rs.

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs, src/core/event.rs

title: Nix flake skips tests (doCheck=false) - CI gap
description:
## What's Wrong
flake.nix has doCheck=false to avoid test failures during nix build. This means `nix build` won't catch test regressions.

## Where
- flake.nix:42-43

## Why It Matters
Users installing via nix won't get test validation. If tests break, nix users get broken builds.

## Options
1. Fix whatever causes tests to fail in nix sandbox (likely git/network related)
2. Add a separate `nix flake check` that runs tests outside the build
3. Accept the gap and rely on CI (current state)

title: Add typestate for WAL records (Seq1 + verified header/body)
description:
**Problem**
RecordHeader stores origin_seq as u64 and Record represents both decoded and validated states. Several paths (repl/wal replay/range reads) can use Record without proving origin_seq >= 1 or header/body/hash consistency. There is a validate_header_matches_body helper, but nothing in types forces callers to use it. This is brittle and violates type_design guidance (invariants should be unrepresentable).

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/record.rs, src/daemon/wal/frame.rs, src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/daemon/repl/runtime.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
design:
**Design**
- Split Record into raw vs verified variants (e.g., Record<Unverified> and Record<Verified>).
- Use Seq1 (or a new nonzero type) in the verified RecordHeader to encode origin_seq >= 1.
- Provide Record::decode_raw + Record::verify_with_event_body (checks header/body match and sha256(event_body_bytes)).
- Update replay/range readers to return Verified records; update writers to build Verified records directly.
- Add targeted tests for seq0 rejection and header/body mismatch errors.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No code path can use a verified Record without Seq1 and header/body/hash checks.
- [ ] Record decode rejects origin_seq=0 early.
- [ ] WAL replay and WAL range reads use the verified record type.
- [ ] Tests cover header/body mismatch and seq0 rejection.

title: Boundary ID/slug type tightening
description:
**Problem**
Some boundary layers still accept raw strings for IDs/slugs, risking invalid values slipping through.

**Design**
Audit CLI/IPC entrypoints and replace raw string handling with strong types (BeadId/BeadSlug/ActorId) as early as possible. Add validation helpers for common parse paths and remove fallible unwraps.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Parsing/validation centralized
- [ ] No new unwraps
- [ ] Tests cover invalid inputs

**Files:** src/cli/*, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/core/identity.rs

title: Tests for CLI read gating parsing and propagation
description:
**Problem**
Read gating is easy to regress; we need tests that lock the JSON parsing and ensure read consistency propagation.

**Design**
Add focused unit tests for parsing require_min_seen JSON and for Ctx::read_consistency propagating require_min_seen + wait_timeout_ms. Keep tests in src/cli/mod.rs where other CLI parsing tests live.

**Files**
- src/cli/mod.rs
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Test for valid require_min_seen JSON parsing
- [ ] Test for invalid JSON returns ValidationFailed
- [ ] Test for Ctx::read_consistency includes require_min_seen and wait_timeout_ms

title: WAL replay mid-file corruption should fail fast
description:
**Problem**
WAL replay truncates on any corruption, including mid-file CRC/record decode errors, because scan_segment doesn't distinguish tail vs mid-file. This violates REALTIME_PLAN.md 5.2 (online vs offline boundary), which requires fail-fast on mid-file corruption and only tail truncation repairs.

**Design**
In src/daemon/wal/replay.rs scan_segment, detect tail vs mid-file:
- tail: remaining < FRAME_HEADER_LEN, frame_len > remaining, or crc mismatch where offset+frame_len == file_len.
- mid-file: return explicit WalReplayError (new variant) with path+offset; do not truncate.
Only truncate in tail cases when repair_tail=true. Surface operator action to run bd store fsck.
Add tests for mid-file corruption (expect error) and tail corruption (truncate) using WAL fixtures.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Mid-file corruption during replay returns error and does not truncate.
- [ ] Tail corruption still truncates and continues.
- [ ] Tests cover mid-file vs tail behavior; cargo test passes.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, tests/phase3_wal.rs

title: Blocking freshness loads stall entire daemon
description:
**Problem**
`ensure_repo_fresh()` does a blocking `GitOp::Load` roundtrip on the state thread. Any slow fetch = every client request pauses. OK-ish for small repos, but a design footgun.

**Design**
Make refresh async:
- If repo is clean and refresh TTL hit, kick off background load
- Serve current cached state meanwhile
- Apply refreshed state when it arrives (maybe invalidate caches)
- Optionally: let queries request `fresh=true` when they really need it

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Slow git fetch doesnt block unrelated client requests
- [ ] Stale reads are bounded (TTL still respected eventually)
- [ ] Fresh flag available for queries that need it

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/query_executor.rs

title: CRDT join property tests (commutative/associative/idempotent)
description:
**Problem**
We don’t have property tests proving CRDT join algebra and invariants hold across random states. A subtle regression could cause silent data loss.

**Design**
Add proptest generators for CanonicalState (beads + tombstones + deps) and assert:
- join(a,b) == join(b,a)
- join(a, join(b,c)) == join(join(a,b), c)
- join(a,a) == a
- invariants hold after join (live ∩ tombstones = ∅, dep indexes consistent)
Use minimal valid field generation (safe ActorId/BeadId) and keep sizes small for speed.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Property tests added and deterministic
- [ ] No panics; invariants checked
- [ ] Tests run in CI

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/core/dep.rs, Cargo.toml

title: Track sealed WAL segments + final_len invariant
description:
**Problem**
wal.sqlite segment rows are always sealed=false and final_len=None; replay does not enforce sealed segment file_len == final_len. REALTIME_PLAN.md 5.2 requires sealed segment invariants and fail-fast on mismatch.

**Design**
When rotating segments, mark the previous segment sealed with final_len (bytes_written). Persist sealed/final_len in wal.sqlite segments table. On startup/replay, if a sealed segment exists and file_len != final_len, return WalReplayError and surface operator action. Add fsck check to flag sealed length mismatch.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Segment rotation updates wal.sqlite segments with sealed=true and final_len.
- [ ] Replay errors on sealed segment length mismatch.
- [ ] Fsck reports sealed length mismatch.
- [ ] Tests cover sealed invariant enforcement; cargo test passes.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/segment.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs, tests/phase3_index.rs

title: Repl gap buffer reject reasons should be typed
description:
**Problem**
`IngestDecision::Reject { code: String }` in `src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs` uses ad-hoc string codes ("prev_unknown", "gap_timeout", "gap_buffer_overflow", etc.) and the session layer forwards them into `SubscriberLagged` errors. This is brittle and makes it easy to add new reasons without updating error handling.

**Files**
- src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs
- src/daemon/repl/session.rs
- src/core/error/details.rs (if adding structured reason)
design: Replace string codes with a typed enum (e.g., `ReplRejectReason`). Map that enum to error payloads (and, if needed, add a structured detail field for the reason). Update gap buffer + session logic and tests accordingly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `Reject` carries a typed reason, not a string.
- [ ] Error payloads include the mapped reason consistently.
- [ ] Gap buffer/session tests updated.

title: Sync fails when migrating from beads-go in PR branch with existing store
description:
**Problem**
When migrating from beads-go to beads-rs in a PR branch, the sync doesn't work properly. Beads appear not to exist after migration.

**Root Cause**
The `migrate from-go` command bypasses the daemon entirely - it directly writes to `refs/heads/beads/store` using `SyncProcess` (src/cli/commands/migrate.rs:71-74). But the daemon caches state in memory per-remote in `RepoState`. After migration completes:
1. Git ref has the migrated state
2. Daemon still has empty/old cached state
3. CLI queries go through daemon, return stale data

**Design**
Add a `Request::Refresh` IPC command that tells the daemon to reload state from the git ref for a given repo. The migrate command should call this after successful migration.

Alternatively, migrate could go through the daemon IPC entirely, but that's more invasive.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] After `bd migrate from-go`, `bd list` shows the migrated beads
- [ ] Works even if daemon was already running before migration
- [ ] Test: migrate in a fresh clone, verify state is visible

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/commands/migrate.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
closed_reason: Fixed: added Request::Refresh and notify daemon after migration

title: Write migration guide for beads-go to beads-rs
description:
Document the migration path for users of the Go version to switch to Rust. Cover:

- Data format changes (if any)
- CLI differences
- New features in beads-rs
- How to migrate existing beads data

This helps adoption and shows the project is mature enough to replace the Go version.

title: Phase 4: add dep/delete ops to TxnDelta + apply
description: MutationEngine currently rejects parent/dependency updates and delete because TxnDeltaV1 + apply_event only cover bead_upsert/note_append. Implement bead_delete + dep_upsert/dep_delete in core wire/event/apply, update CBOR encoding/decoding, and wire mutation planning to support create-with-deps, add/remove dep, set parent, delete.

title: WAL write failure must abort mutation + preserve state
description:
**Problem**
Mutations can succeed even when WAL writes fail. In `src/daemon/core.rs` `mark_dirty_and_schedule` logs a warning and proceeds, so a crash can lose acknowledged data. There is no rollback of in-memory state when WAL writes fail.

**Design**
Introduce a transactional mutation path that applies changes to a cloned `CanonicalState`, writes the WAL entry first, and only commits the new state + dirty flag on success. Add a dedicated OpError (e.g., `WalError`/`DurabilityError`) surfaced via IPC. Ensure WAL sequence only advances on successful write. Add a unit test that forces WAL write failure and verifies no state change + error return.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL write failures return an error and do not modify repo state
- [ ] WAL sequence/dirty flags remain unchanged on WAL failure
- [ ] Successful WAL writes still schedule sync
- [ ] Tests cover WAL failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/ops.rs, src/daemon/wal.rs

title: BroadcastEvent should not require canonical bytes (type-state leak)
description:
**Problem**
`BroadcastEvent` requires `EventBytes<Canonical>`, but replication ingest accepts `EventBytes<Opaque>` and may forward non‑canonical CBOR bytes (allowed in v0.5). In `ingest_remote_batch` we wrap opaque bytes as canonical, so the type lies about the invariant.

**Signals / Evidence**
- `BroadcastEvent.bytes: EventBytes<Canonical>` (`src/daemon/broadcast.rs`).
- Remote ingest constructs `BroadcastEvent` by wrapping `EventBytes<Opaque>` into `EventBytes<Canonical>` (`src/daemon/core.rs`).
- `EventBytes<Canonical>::new` is public, so any caller can claim canonicality without validation.

**Why this hurts velocity**
This is a classic “types lying” case. It makes it impossible to reason about whether bytes are canonical and forces reviewers to audit every call site instead of trusting the type.
design:
**Design**
- Change `BroadcastEvent` to carry `EventBytes<Opaque>` (or add an enum/marker for `Canonical | Opaque`).
- Only label bytes as canonical when produced by the canonical encoder.
